children.name: Sylva Identity Discovery (T)
children.description: Open-source CLI tool for username and identity discovery with branch discovery to expand searches as additional linked identities are uncovered.
children.bestFor: Username enumeration with identity branching
children.output: Linked accounts and identities across platforms
children.opsecNote: Makes requests to target platforms to check username existence; external API keys may be used.

children.name: Sherlock (T)
children.description: Python CLI tool that hunts down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks. Supports Tor routing, proxy configuration, and CSV/XLSX export.
children.bestFor: Mass username enumeration across 400+ sites
children.output: List of discovered profile URLs across social networks
children.opsecNote: Directly queries each target site to check username existence; supports Tor/proxy for anonymity.

children.name: Namechk
children.description: Web-based username and domain availability checker that searches 100+ social media platforms and 36 domain extensions simultaneously.
children.bestFor: Quick username availability check across social media and domains
children.output: Availability status across 100+ platforms and domain extensions
children.opsecNote: Searches are routed through Namechk's servers; target accounts are not directly contacted by the user.

children.name: WhatsMyName (T)
children.description: OSINT project maintaining a curated JSON database of website detection patterns for username enumeration. Web interface available at whatsmyname.app.
children.bestFor: Username enumeration using community-maintained site detection data
children.output: List of sites where the username exists, based on HTTP response pattern matching
children.opsecNote: Makes HTTP requests to each target site to check for username existence.

children.name: Thats Them
children.description: Free people search engine aggregating data from 50+ sources. Supports lookups by name, address, phone number, or email.
children.bestFor: People search by name, email, phone, or address
children.input: Name, email address, phone number, or physical address
children.output: Contact info, residential details, demographics, and financial estimates
children.opsecNote: Searches are routed through ThatsThem's servers; the target is not alerted.

children.name: NameCheckup
children.description: Free web-based username and domain availability checker that searches across 20+ social media platforms and 40+ domain extensions with WHOIS lookup support.
children.bestFor: Username and domain availability checking with WHOIS info
children.output: Availability status across social platforms and domain extensions, with WHOIS data
children.opsecNote: Searches are proxied through NameCheckup's servers; no direct contact with target platforms by the user.

children.name: GitFive (T)
children.description: OSINT CLI tool for investigating GitHub profiles. Tracks username/name history, maps emails to accounts, extracts SSH public keys, and exports findings as JSON.
children.bestFor: Deep investigation of GitHub user profiles and email-to-account mapping
children.output: Profile history, linked emails, SSH keys, repository analysis, JSON export
children.opsecNote: Queries GitHub API directly; developer recommends using a secondary GitHub account.

children.name: Sherlock
children.description: Python CLI tool that hunts down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks. Supports Tor routing, proxy configuration, and CSV/XLSX export.
children.bestFor: Mass username enumeration across 400+ sites
children.output: List of discovered profile URLs across social networks
children.opsecNote: Directly queries each target site to check username existence; supports Tor/proxy for anonymity.

children.name: Names Directory
children.description: Searchable database of 1B+ name combinations collected from public sources. Allows bidirectional lookup to find first names by surname or surnames by first name.
children.bestFor: Finding name combinations and frequency data for a given first or last name
children.output: Associated name combinations with frequency counts
children.opsecNote: Queries a static public database; no contact with any target individual.

children.name: Lullar
children.description: Free people search and username lookup tool that searches across 148+ social media platforms including Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and LinkedIn.
children.bestFor: Social media profile discovery by username, email, or name
children.input: Email address, full name, or username
children.output: Social media profiles found across 148+ platforms
children.opsecNote: Searches are routed through Lullar's servers; no direct queries from the user to target platforms.

children.name: Amazon Usernames (M)
children.description: Google dork that searches Amazon.com for pages associated with a specific username, surfacing public profiles, wishlists, and reviews.
children.bestFor: Finding Amazon public profiles, wishlists, and review activity by username
children.output: Google search results linking to Amazon pages mentioning the username
children.opsecNote: Query goes to Google, not Amazon directly. Google may log the search but the target is not alerted.

children.name: Github User (M)
children.description: Queries the GitHub public Events API to retrieve a user's recent public activity, including pushes, pull requests, issues, and other repository events.
children.bestFor: Enumerating a GitHub user's recent public activity and repository interactions
children.output: JSON array of public events (pushes, PRs, issues, comments) with timestamps and repo details
children.opsecNote: Unauthenticated API call; GitHub rate-limits by IP (60 req/hr) but does not notify the target user.

children.name: Tinder Usernames (M)
children.description: Accesses a Tinder user's public web profile via their username. The gotinder.com domain redirects to tinder.com.
children.bestFor: Confirming existence of a Tinder profile and viewing public profile details
children.output: Public profile page with name, photo, and basic info if the user has web sharing enabled
children.opsecNote: Simple HTTP GET to a public page; target is not notified of profile views via the web URL.

children.name: Keybase
children.description: Platform for cryptographic identity verification, linking social media accounts, PGP keys, and cryptocurrency addresses to a single profile. Acquired by Zoom in 2020 but still operational.
children.bestFor: Looking up verified social accounts, PGP keys, and crypto wallets tied to a username
children.output: User profile showing verified identities across platforms, PGP keys, cryptocurrency addresses, and devices
children.opsecNote: Public profile lookups are passive web requests. No login required to view profiles.

children.name: MIT PGP Key Server
children.description: MIT PGP Public Key Server for searching, submitting, and removing PGP public keys. Look up keys by name, email, or key ID to find associated cryptographic identities.
children.bestFor: Looking up PGP public keys associated with a username or email address
children.input: Name, email address, or key ID
children.output: PGP public key data, key fingerprints, associated UIDs/email addresses, and key metadata
children.opsecNote: Public key server query; no authentication required and target is not notified of lookups.

children.name: ProtonMail users (M)
children.description: Queries ProtonMail's HKP-compatible PGP key server to look up the public key for a ProtonMail username. A successful response confirms the account exists.
children.bestFor: Confirming whether a ProtonMail username exists and retrieving its PGP public key
children.output: PGP key index with public key fingerprint, algorithm, creation timestamp, and email UID
children.opsecNote: Unauthenticated API query to ProtonMail's public key server. Target user is not notified. Enables user enumeration.

children.name: ProtonMail Domains (M)
children.description: Queries ProtonMail's HKP key server with a full email address to check for a PGP public key. Useful for identifying ProtonMail users on custom domains.
children.bestFor: Checking if an email address on a custom domain is hosted on ProtonMail
children.input: Full email address (any domain that may be hosted on ProtonMail)
children.output: PGP key index with public key fingerprint, algorithm, creation timestamp, and email UID
children.opsecNote: Unauthenticated public HKP endpoint. Target is not notified. Can reveal whether a custom domain uses ProtonMail.

children.name: theHarvester (T)
children.description: Command-line tool for gathering emails, subdomains, IPs, and URLs from public sources.
children.bestFor: Email harvesting, subdomain enumeration, passive recon
children.output: Email addresses, subdomains, IPs, URLs
children.opsecNote: Queries third-party search engines and APIs. Does not contact the target directly.

children.name: Have I been pwned?
children.description: Database of breached credentials and email addresses from known data breaches.
children.bestFor: Breach detection, credential exposure checks
children.input: Email address, phone number, password hash
children.output: Breach names, breach dates, exposed data types
children.opsecNote: Queries breach database via API. Target is not notified of lookups.

children.name: Recon-ng (T)
children.description: Full-featured web reconnaissance framework with independent modules for data gathering.
children.bestFor: Modular web recon, API-driven data collection
children.output: Contacts, hosts, credentials, ports via module-specific results
children.opsecNote: Queries third-party APIs and data sources. Does not probe the target unless specific modules are configured to do so.

children.name: theHarvester (T)
children.description: Command-line tool for gathering emails, subdomains, IPs, and URLs from public sources.
children.bestFor: Email harvesting, subdomain enumeration, passive recon
children.output: Email addresses, subdomains, IPs, URLs
children.opsecNote: Queries third-party search engines and APIs. Does not contact the target directly.

children.name: Shodan
children.description: Search engine for internet-connected devices. Find open ports, services, and vulnerabilities across the public internet.
children.bestFor: Port scanning, banner grabbing, IoT discovery
children.output: Open ports, services, banners, CVEs
children.opsecNote: Queries cached scan data. Does not directly probe the target.

children.name: BuiltWith
children.description: Technology profiler that identifies the tech stack, analytics, and frameworks used by websites.
children.bestFor: Technology stack identification, competitor analysis
children.output: Technology list, analytics IDs, hosting info, historical tech changes
children.opsecNote: Queries cached technology profiles. Does not contact the target.

children.name: Censys
children.description: Internet-wide scanner and search engine for hosts, certificates, and services.
children.bestFor: Certificate discovery, host enumeration, exposure monitoring
children.input: Domain, IP, certificate fingerprint, search query
children.output: Host details, open ports, TLS certificates, service banners
children.opsecNote: Queries pre-scanned data. Does not probe the target directly.

children.name: BuiltWith
children.description: Technology profiler that identifies the tech stack, analytics, and frameworks used by websites.
children.bestFor: Technology stack identification, competitor analysis
children.output: Technology list, analytics IDs, hosting info, historical tech changes
children.opsecNote: Queries cached technology profiles. Does not contact the target.

children.name: theHarvester (T)
children.description: Command-line tool for gathering emails, subdomains, IPs, and URLs from public sources.
children.bestFor: Email harvesting, subdomain enumeration, passive recon
children.output: Email addresses, subdomains, IPs, URLs
children.opsecNote: Queries third-party search engines and APIs. Does not contact the target directly.

children.name: SpiderFoot (T)
children.description: Automated OSINT collection tool with 200+ modules for reconnaissance and threat intelligence.
children.bestFor: Automated recon, attack surface mapping, threat intelligence
children.input: Domain, IP, email, name, phone, subnet
children.output: Correlated intelligence graph, structured findings across modules
children.opsecNote: Some modules actively probe targets. Review module settings before running.

children.name: xeuledoc - Fetch metadata about any public Google document
Print Storyboard from Youtube

";}var title=ytplayer.config.args.title;msg="

children.name: Fedifinder
children.description: Web tool that scanned Twitter profiles to find Fediverse/Mastodon handles among your contacts and exported them as CSV. The hosted instance now returns HTTP 410 and is no longer functional due to Twitter API restrictions.
children.bestFor: Finding Twitter contacts who moved to Mastodon/Fediverse
children.output: CSV list of discovered Fediverse handles from your Twitter contacts
children.opsecNote: Requires Twitter OAuth authentication; scans public bios and tweets of followed accounts.

children.name: Fediverse Observer
children.description: Real-time dashboard tracking Fediverse instances across Mastodon, Pleroma, Misskey, PeerTube, and other ActivityPub platforms with server statistics and geographic mapping.
children.bestFor: Discovering and mapping Fediverse instances by software, country, or size
children.input: Search filters (software type, country, language, instance name)
children.output: Instance list with user counts, uptime, software version, registration status, and geographic location
children.opsecNote: Queries the Fediverse Observer database, not individual instances; no contact with target servers.

children.name: Fediverse_OSINT (T)
children.description: Python CLI tool for checking whether a domain belongs to the Fediverse and hunting usernames across discoverable Fediverse servers.
children.bestFor: Cross-instance Fediverse user and content search
children.output: User profiles and posts found across Fediverse instances
children.opsecNote: Queries multiple Fediverse instances directly; requests may be logged by instance administrators.

children.name: Masto (T)
children.description: Python-based Mastodon OSINT tool for investigating user accounts across instances. Retrieves profile details, toots, followers, and account metadata.
children.bestFor: Mastodon user profile investigation and account analysis
children.output: Profile details, recent toots, follower/following lists, account creation date, and metadata
children.opsecNote: Makes API requests directly to the target Mastodon instance; instance admins can see request logs.

children.name: TikTok (M)
children.description: Direct TikTok profile lookup by manually editing the username in the URL. Quick way to check if a target has a TikTok presence.
children.bestFor: Quick TikTok profile existence check
children.output: Public TikTok profile page with videos, bio, and follower counts
children.opsecNote: Standard web request to TikTok; profile views are not visible to the account owner without login.

children.name: TikTok-OSINT (T)
children.description: Python CLI tool for collecting OSINT data from TikTok profiles including user metadata, video listings, and engagement statistics. Last updated October 2022.
children.bestFor: Automated TikTok profile data collection
children.output: User metadata, video listings, engagement stats, and follower data
children.opsecNote: Makes direct API requests to TikTok servers; may trigger rate limiting.

children.name: Unfurl
children.description: Web and CLI tool that extracts and visualizes metadata embedded in URLs, including TikTok share links, timestamps, and tracking parameters. Also installable locally via Python.
children.bestFor: URL metadata extraction and timestamp analysis
children.output: Visual graph of extracted URL components, timestamps, and embedded identifiers
children.opsecNote: Parses URL structure locally in browser or on the Unfurl server; does not contact the target platform. Local install available for sensitive investigations.

children.name: yt-dlp (T)
children.description: Feature-rich command-line video downloader supporting 1000+ sites including TikTok. Extracts video files, metadata, thumbnails, subtitles, and comments.
children.bestFor: Downloading TikTok videos and extracting metadata for preservation
children.input: TikTok video URL or user profile URL
children.output: Downloaded video files, JSON metadata, thumbnails, subtitles, and comments
children.opsecNote: Makes direct HTTP requests to TikTok to download content; IP is visible to the platform.

children.name: Bellingcat Meta Content Library
children.description: Bellingcat's guide to Meta's Content Library, a research platform providing near-real-time searchable archive of public Threads, Facebook, and Instagram content.
children.bestFor: Large-scale Threads content search for academic and non-profit research
children.input: Search queries, date ranges, content filters
children.output: Public posts, engagement metrics, and text-in-image search results from Threads and other Meta platforms
children.opsecNote: Searches Meta's research archive; does not interact with target accounts directly. Requires academic/non-profit eligibility approval.

children.name: Threads Dashboard
children.description: Analytics and insights platform for Threads accounts using the official API. Tracks audience demographics, engagement metrics, and historical posting data.
children.bestFor: Threads account analytics and engagement investigation
children.output: Audience demographics, engagement rates, posting frequency, optimal posting times, and historical data
children.opsecNote: Retrieves data via Meta's official Threads API; no direct contact with the target account.

children.name: Threads-Scraper (T)
children.description: Python browser automation tool that scrapes public Threads posts and profiles without authentication, outputting structured data in JSON, CSV, or XML. Last updated July 2023.
children.bestFor: Bulk extraction of Threads posts for offline analysis
children.input: Threads profile URL or post URL
children.output: Extracted posts in JSON, CSV, or XML format with metadata
children.opsecNote: Scrapes Threads directly; depends on site structure and may trigger rate limiting.

children.name: ThreadsRecon (T)
children.description: Python OSINT tool for Threads profile analysis including sentiment analysis, network visualization, and automated PDF reporting.
children.bestFor: Threads profile investigation with sentiment and network analysis
children.output: Profile analysis, sentiment scores, network graphs, and PDF investigation reports
children.opsecNote: Makes direct requests to Threads to collect profile and post data for analysis.

children.name: InfoFlow Public People Search In Chilean
The World Bank Open Data Catalog
US Nav Guide Zip Code Data
PubMed - National Center for Biotechnology Information
Anna's Archive
children.description: Meta-search index for books and papers that aggregates links from multiple shadow libraries.
children.bestFor: Locating mirrored copies of books and papers from multiple sources
children.input: Book title, author, ISBN, DOI, or keyword
children.output: Indexed records with download and mirror links
children.opsecNote: Searches are query-based and may be logged; accessing mirrored content can carry legal and operational risk.

children.name: Archive.is
children.description: On-demand web snapshot service that preserves point-in-time copies of pages and shortens archive links.
children.bestFor: Capturing and retrieving snapshots of volatile web pages
children.output: Archived page copy with permanent archive URL
children.opsecNote: Reading existing snapshots is passive; submitting new captures is visible to the archive provider.

children.name: Browsershots
children.description: Legacy cross-browser screenshot service historically used to render pages in multiple browser/OS combinations.
children.bestFor: Historical reference for legacy browser rendering captures
children.output: Rendered webpage screenshots
children.opsecNote: Service is effectively discontinued; requests are unlikely to complete.

children.name: Cached Pages
children.description: Web cache lookup utility that surfaces archived and cached versions of a target page from multiple sources.
children.bestFor: Finding recent cached copies of pages that changed or disappeared
children.output: Links to cached or archived snapshots
children.opsecNote: Queries third-party caches and archives without contacting the target site directly.

children.name: Cached View
children.description: Simple cache-checking service that retrieves copies of pages from search engine and archive caches.
children.bestFor: Quick verification of whether a removed page still exists in cache
children.output: Cached page links from available providers
children.opsecNote: Lookup activity is handled by the cache service and does not interact with the target host directly.

children.name: Common Crawl
children.description: Open repository of large-scale web crawl data published as monthly WARC datasets.
children.bestFor: Large-scale historical web content mining and corpus analysis
children.input: CC index query, URL, domain, or WARC request
children.output: Raw crawl records, metadata indexes, and extracted web content
children.opsecNote: Reads published crawl datasets and indexes; no direct interaction with target systems.

children.name: Internet Archive: Wayback Machine
children.description: Web archive providing historical snapshots of websites captured over time.
children.bestFor: Historical website analysis and deleted content recovery
children.output: Archived web page snapshots with timestamps
children.opsecNote: Queries archived data. Does not contact the target. Searches may be logged by the Internet Archive.

children.name: PDF My URL
children.description: Converts web pages into downloadable PDF captures for documentation and evidence preservation.
children.bestFor: Generating quick PDF evidence captures of web pages
children.output: PDF snapshot of page content
children.opsecNote: Third-party conversion service processes submitted URLs; target is typically fetched by the service backend.

children.name: Screenshots.com
children.description: Website screenshot archive and capture service for viewing historical or current rendered page images.
children.bestFor: Visual timeline checks of website appearance changes
children.output: Stored website screenshots and capture previews
children.opsecNote: Operational status requires follow-up verification; treat availability as uncertain.

children.name: Wayback Machine Chrome Extension
children.description: Browser extension that detects missing pages and loads historical versions from the Wayback Machine.
children.bestFor: Fast archive lookups while browsing dead or changed pages
children.input: Current tab URL or missing page request
children.output: Direct Wayback snapshot links from browser context
children.opsecNote: Extension requests archive data through Internet Archive endpoints and may log usage telemetry.

children.name: Textfiles.com
children.description: Historic repository preserving early internet text artifacts including BBS files, documents, and underground zines.
children.bestFor: Researching legacy digital culture and historical text archives
children.input: Keyword, directory path, or file browsing
children.output: Archived plain-text files and scanned historical documents
children.opsecNote: Read-only archive browsing; minimal operational exposure beyond normal web access logging.

children.name: UK Web Archive
children.description: British Library-led archive preserving selected UK websites and related national web heritage content.
children.bestFor: Accessing preserved UK web content and historical domain captures
children.output: Archived UK website records and preserved snapshots
children.opsecNote: Service availability has been unstable following cyberattack-related disruptions.

children.name: Waybackpack (T)
children.description: Command-line tool for bulk downloading archived captures from the Internet Archive Wayback Machine.
children.bestFor: Batch export of historical snapshots for offline analysis
children.output: Downloaded archive files and URL/capture lists
children.opsecNote: Automated queries hit archive APIs and can generate identifiable traffic patterns if used at scale.

children.name: Web Archive-RU
children.description: Regional web archiving service focused on preserving and browsing snapshots of selected websites.
children.bestFor: Supplemental archive checks when mainstream archives lack coverage
children.output: Available archived pages and snapshot listings
children.opsecNote: Operational status requires follow-up verification; use as secondary source until confirmed.

children.name: WebCite
children.description: Citation-focused web preservation service with legacy archived records and limited query capabilities.
children.bestFor: Retrieving historical citation captures that still remain accessible
children.input: Archived URL, DOI, or query string
children.output: Stored citation snapshots and metadata records
children.opsecNote: Service appears read-only and partially unavailable; expect incomplete retrieval.

children.name: Cryptome
children.description: Long-running disclosure archive hosting leaked or hard-to-find government, intelligence, and policy documents.
children.bestFor: Searching historical leaked documents and primary-source disclosures
children.output: Published leak archives and document files
children.opsecNote: Read-only document access with standard web logging by host infrastructure.

children.name: Databases.Today
children.description: Breach data discovery portal indexing exposed databases and leaked credential collections.
children.bestFor: Identifying whether target identifiers appear in known breach dumps
children.output: Indexed breach hit results and source references
children.opsecNote: Operational status requires verification; querying breach portals can create attribution and compliance risk.

children.name: WikiLeaks
children.description: Global document leak publication platform containing diplomatic, military, and corporate disclosures.
children.bestFor: Searching high-impact leaked document collections for historical context
children.input: Keyword, topic, date range, or document identifier
children.output: Leaked document pages, files, and related publication context
children.opsecNote: Platform reliability is partial and interactions may carry legal, policy, and attribution exposure.

children.name: Labeled Faces in the Wild DB
children.description: Academic benchmark dataset of labeled face photographs collected from the public web.
children.bestFor: Facial recognition benchmarking and person-image research baselines
children.output: Labeled face image dataset files and metadata
children.opsecNote: Static academic dataset retrieval with no direct target interaction.

children.name: Stanford Large Network Dataset Collection
children.description: SNAP repository of graph/network datasets spanning social networks, web graphs, and communication systems.
children.bestFor: Network analysis modeling and graph-based OSINT experimentation
children.output: Downloadable graph datasets and documentation
children.opsecNote: Public academic data access only; no interaction with investigation targets.

children.name: UCI Spambase Data Set
children.description: Classic UCI machine-learning dataset for spam classification and email feature analysis.
children.bestFor: Email spam model benchmarking and feature-engineering practice
children.input: Dataset page access or download request
children.output: Tabular spam classification dataset and metadata
children.opsecNote: Public dataset retrieval only with negligible operational exposure.

children.name: Visual Genome
children.description: Structured image dataset linking objects, attributes, and scene graph relationships for visual understanding research.
children.bestFor: Image relationship analysis and multimodal data research
children.output: Annotated image corpus with objects, regions, and relation graphs
children.opsecNote: Academic dataset access with no direct investigative target interaction.

children.name: TV Closed Caption Search
children.description: Internet Archive TV News collection for searching closed-caption text across broadcast recordings.
children.bestFor: Finding broadcast mentions by keyword and time period
children.input: Keyword, program, channel, or date range
children.output: Matching TV clips, transcripts, and broadcast metadata
children.opsecNote: Queries public archived broadcasts without interacting with monitored subjects.

children.name: Paterva / Maltego (T)
children.description: Visual link analysis tool for mapping relationships between people, companies, domains, and infrastructure.
children.bestFor: Link analysis, relationship mapping, entity correlation
children.input: Domain, email, IP, name, phone number
children.output: Entity relationship graph, linked records, transform results
children.opsecNote: Transforms may query targets directly. Some data sources log lookups.

children.name: VirusTotal
children.description: Multi-engine file and URL scanner that aggregates results from 70+ antivirus engines and threat feeds.
children.bestFor: Malware analysis, URL reputation, file hash lookups
children.input: File, file hash, URL, domain, IP address
children.output: Detection results, behavioral analysis, community comments, related indicators
children.opsecNote: Uploaded files become visible to other VirusTotal users. Hash lookups are private.
